agent.runner_summary: The simulator builds one Store and LangGraph checkpointer, compiles build_tutor_graph once, preserves a single thread_id for the run, and calls the sync graph through a worker thread on each user turn.

components.when_to_use: Use for wrong welcome-back summaries, learner state snapshots, or missing resume metadata.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"learner_id": "...", "db_path": "..."}) when reproducing a specific learner record; omit kwargs to use the simulator defaults. This wrapper takes only named kwargs.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned JSON for level, lesson pointer, due_vocab, weak_spots, and last_session fields.

components.when_to_use: Use for curriculum progression bugs or when the tutor picks the wrong next lesson.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"learner_id": "...", "db_path": "..."}) to point at the learner state that should drive unit selection. Do not pass positional args.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned JSON for the chosen unit_id, done flag, and level alignment.

components.when_to_use: Use for placement regressions or direct level-setting behavior without running the full graph.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"level": "A2", "learner_id": "...", "db_path": "..."}). The level argument should be one of A1, A2, B1, or B2.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned JSON for ok=true and the normalized level value.

components.when_to_use: Use for resume failures where the tutor should replay a cached lesson but instead regenerates or loses lesson content.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"unit_id": "A1.U1", "learner_id": "...", "db_path": "..."}). The wrapper expects named kwargs only.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate whether the returned JSON lesson is present, structurally valid, and matches the expected unit content.

components.when_to_use: Use for spaced-repetition scheduling regressions after a learner grades a vocab card.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"card_id": 1, "quality": 4, "learner_id": "...", "db_path": "..."}) with named schema args matching the LangChain tool signature.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned JSON for the reviewed lemma, new interval, and due_date changes.

log_notes: User turns appear as user_message events.
Assistant replies appear as agent_message events after the LangGraph run completes for the turn.
LangChain tool invocations appear as tool_call and tool_result events when the graph executes tool-enabled specialist agents.
Observed RELAI tool mocks appear as mock_call events on the same turn.
learning_env_guidance: Use an agent target for routing, guardrail, specialist-selection, or multi-turn conversation regressions.
Use a component target when the bug is isolated to learner-state lookup, curriculum progression, cached lesson retrieval, direct level changes, or spaced-repetition updates.
Use FixedInput when exact commands such as /lesson, /resume, /progress, or /level A2 matter to the regression.
Use PersonaInput when learner confusion, persistence, or conversational tone changes the router or specialist behavior.
Use LLMJudgeEvaluator for teaching quality, level appropriateness, and refusal quality; use CodeEvaluator for deterministic JSON outputs, selected tool calls, and persisted-state expectations.
